message.content: Adversaries may exploit serverless cloud infrastructure, such as Cloudflare Workers or AWS Lambda functions, to obscure their activities and complicate attribution. By compromising these environments, they can either directly interact with infected machines or proxy traffic to their command and control servers. This technique leverages the inherent trust and ubiquity of cloud provider subdomains, making malicious traffic appear as legitimate cloud service traffic, thus evading detection. Common exploitation methods include deploying malicious code within serverless functions to execute commands or relay communications. Mitigation strategies involve implementing strict access controls, monitoring for unusual activity within serverless environments, and employing network traffic analysis to identify anomalies. Regular audits and adherence to cloud security best practices are essential to reduce the risk of such compromises.
